content:
# Revelation Chapter 22 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE, pages 294–295 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/REV_022_page_001.jpg, REV_022_page_002.jpg

1. And he showed me a river of water of life, bright as crystal, proceeding out of the throne of God and of the Lamb.

2. In [the] middle of her street, and on this side and on that side of the river, [was the] tree of life producing twelve fruits, according to each month yielding its fruit. And the leaves of the tree [are] for [the] healing of the nations.

3. And every curse will be no more. And the throne of God and of the Lamb will be in her, and his servants will serve him,

4. and they will see his face, and his name [will be] upon their foreheads.

5. And night will be no more, and they have no need of light of lamp and light of sun, because [the] Lord God will shine upon them, and they will reign unto the ages of the ages.

6. And he said to me, "These words [are] faithful and true; and the Lord, the God of the spirits of the prophets, sent his angel to show to his servants the things which must come to pass quickly."

7. "And behold, I am coming quickly. Blessed [is] the one keeping the words of the prophecy of this book."

8. And I, Yoannes, [am] the one hearing and seeing these things. And when I heard and saw, I fell to worship before the feet of the angel showing me these things.

9. And he says to me, "See [that you do] not! I am a fellow-servant of you and of your brothers the prophets and of those keeping the words of this book. Worship God."

10. And he says to me, "Do not seal the words of the prophecy of this book, for the time is near.

11. The one doing unrighteousness, let him do unrighteousness still; and the filthy one, let him be filthy still; and the righteous one, let him do righteousness still; and the holy one, let him be holy still."

12. "Behold, I am coming quickly, and my reward [is] with me, to render to each one as his work is.

13. I [am] the Alpha and the Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end."

14. Blessed [are] those washing their robes, so that their authority will be over the tree of life and by the gates they may enter into the city.

15. Outside [are] the dogs and the sorcerers and the fornicators and the murderers and the idolaters, and everyone loving and doing falsehood.

16. "I, Yeshua, sent my angel to testify to you these things for the churches. I am the root and the offspring of David, the bright morning star."

17. And the Spirit and the bride say, "Come." And the one hearing, let him say, "Come." And the one thirsting, let him come; the one desiring, let him take [the] water of life freely.

18. I testify to everyone hearing the words of the prophecy of this book: if anyone adds upon them, God will add upon him the plagues having been written in this book;

19. and if anyone takes away from the words of the book of this prophecy, God will take away his part from the tree of life and out of the holy city, the things having been written in this book.

20. The one testifying these things says, "Yes, I am coming quickly." Amen. Come, Lord Yeshua.

21. The grace of the Lord Yeshua [be] with all the holy ones. Amen.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — "bright as crystal":** lampron hōs krustallon — Sinaiticus uses lampron (bright/radiant), modifying the water, not the river. The water itself shines.

2. **v.2 — "tree of life":** xulon zōēs — literally "wood of life." The singular xulon (tree/wood) with the river on both sides has generated much discussion. Sinaiticus reads the singular, envisioning one tree spanning or accessible from both banks.

3. **v.3 — "every curse":** pan katathema — Sinaiticus reads katathema (curse/accursed thing), not katanathema. This is the milder form, though the meaning is equivalent in context. The curse of Genesis 3 is here undone.

4. **v.5 — "light of lamp and light of sun":** Sinaiticus reads phōs luchnou kai phōs hēliou — the double "light of" construction. Some manuscripts omit the second phōs. EMET preserves the Sinaiticus repetition.

5. **v.8 — "Yoannes":** Iōannēs — Sinaiticus spelling. EMET transliterates as "Yoannes" to reflect the Greek form as written in the manuscript, preserving the Semitic root (Yohanan).

6. **v.11 — Fourfold imperative:** Sinaiticus preserves four parallel commands — to the unrighteous, the filthy, the righteous, and the holy. This is an eschatological fixing of character, not an encouragement to sin. The aorist imperatives suggest decisive, final action.

7. **v.14 — "washing their robes":** plunontes tas stolas autōn — Sinaiticus reads "those washing their robes," not "those doing his commandments" (poiountes tas entolas autou) as in the Textus Receptus. This is a significant textual variant. The washing-of-robes reading connects to 7:14 and the blood of the Lamb.

8. **v.14 — "their authority will be over the tree of life":** hina estai hē exousia autōn epi to xulon tēs zōēs — literally "so that their authority will be upon the tree of life." The preposition epi with the tree suggests rightful access, not mere permission.

9. **v.17 — Triple "Come":** The Spirit and the bride issue the invitation; the hearer is to echo it; and the thirsty one is to respond. This threefold structure is the penultimate appeal of the entire Bible.

10. **v.19 — "tree of life":** Sinaiticus reads tou xulou tēs zōēs ("the tree of life"), not tou bibliou tēs zōēs ("the book of life") as in some later manuscripts. This is a major variant — the punishment for tampering with this book is loss of access to the tree and the city, not erasure from a book.

11. **v.21 — "with all the holy ones":** meta pantōn tōn hagiōn — Sinaiticus reads "with all the holy ones" (saints). Some manuscripts read "with all" (meta pantōn), others "with you" (meth' hymōn). The Sinaiticus reading is the most expansive benediction, encompassing all the sanctified. This is the final word of the Christian Bible in the Sinaiticus manuscript.

12. **Final note — End of the Canon:** With verse 21, the Codex Sinaiticus concludes the Book of Revelation and thus the entire New Testament. The colophon in the manuscript reads APOKALYPSIS IOANNOU ("Revelation of John"). What follows in Sinaiticus is the Epistle of Barnabas and the Shepherd of Hermas, works ultimately excluded from the canon. The Bible, as received, ends here: with grace, with the holy ones, and with Amen.

— EMET, AI Translator
